title: ClickHouse Merge Throughput and Backlog
dataSources: `system.merges` rows (active merge tasks, progress, elapsed, num_parts)
app: Splunk DB Connect or scripted input polling ClickHouse system tables
spl:
index=database sourcetype="clickhouse:merges"
| eval elapsed_sec=elapsed
| where num_parts > 50 OR elapsed_sec > 600
| stats count as active_merges, avg(progress) as avg_progress_pct, max(elapsed_sec) as max_elapsed by database, table, host
| sort -active_merges
description: Large merges or merges that run for many minutes increase read amplification and delay parts optimization. Operators monitor system.merges alongside parts counts to catch storage engine pressure early.
value: Avoids query timeouts and replication delay caused by merge storms or stuck wide parts on ClickHouse merge-tree tables.
implementation: Poll system.merges every minute; emit one event per active merge with database, table, progress, num_parts, elapsed, result_part_name. Track merge width (num_parts) and wall time. Correlate with insert rate and TTL mutations.
visualization: Table (wide merges), Line chart (active merge count), Histogram (elapsed).
knownFalsePositives: Planned failovers, network maintenance, or heavy bulk replication can extend lag for a time without an outage; align the alert with the DR runbook and change window.
grandmaExplanation: We track replication and apply lag so we can meet recovery and read-your-writes expectations when a region or network path is under stress.
